rulings.Case Title: MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION VS. WHOLE FOODS MARKET, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Inspection And Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 1 - PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION's Motion To Compel Inspection And Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful for the parties to be prepared to address the status of their negotiations concerning a stipulated consent judgment. The parties shall meet and confer prior to the hearing and provide the court with an agreed-upon date for a continued hearing on Plaintiff's motion in the event the parties are unable to reach a final written settlement. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HANS REINHARDT VS. PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Prana Eight Properties Llc'S Motion For Protective Order To Limit Written Discovery Propounded By Plaintiff.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 2 - DEFENDANT PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES LLC's Motion For Protective Order To Limit Written Discovery Propounded By Plaintiff. (Part 2) GRANTED IN PART. The only cause of action remaining in the Second Amended Complaint after the court's May 6, 2013 order is the fifth cause of action alleging that Defendant Prana breached the implied warranty of habitability by allowing widespread bedbug infestation at the Biltmore Hotel. Plaintiff seeks discovery that is not relevant to the subject matter involved in that cause of action. Defendant need not respond to the following discovery: Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One, Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - Employment, Set One; Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 1 - 24 & 26; Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions - Truth of Facts, Set One, Nos. 1 through 97, 105, 109 through 114, 121, 123 through 129; 142 through163, 166, 167, 169 & 170; Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions - Genuineness of Documents, Set One, Nos. 134 through149; Plaintiff's Requests for Production, Set One, Nos. 1 through 45, 47 through 79, 81 through 87, 95 through 104; Requests for Admissions - Truth of Facts, Set Two, Nos. 9 through 14, 24 through 29, 30 through 66, 81 through 101; Plaintiff's Request for Production, Set Two, Nos. 21 through 24, 26, 28, 30, 32, 33 through 36, 41 through 65, 70 through 74; Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions - Genuineness of Documents, Set Two, Nos. 1 through 8, 11 through 25, 27 through 34, 80 through 83, 86, 98 through 104; Plaintiff shall choose a maximum of 35 RFAs - truth of facts that are not on the above lists. Defendant shall then respond to the 35 requests. Defendant shall respond to the portions of the other discovery requests not enumerated above, as well as to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer, Set One, and Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - General, Set Two., except that the words "termination of employment at Biltmore Hotel on May 31, 2011 are stricken from the definition of "Incident," and the term "Incident" is limited to "Plaintiff's complaint about breach of habitability." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order For 1) Interrogatory Requests In Excess Of Thirty-Five Without Necessity 2) Medical And Financial Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 3 - PLAINTIFF SHELLEY KRAMER's Motion For A Protective Order For 1) Interrogatory Requests In Excess Of Thirty-Five Without Necessity 2) Medical And Financial Documents. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE B.H. DEVELOPMENT, INC. VS. DONALD KELLEBREW DBA D&K CONSTRUCTION & DEVELOPMEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT ADOBE WEST LANDSCAPING COMPANY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted, for good cause shown. Defendant has established mistake pursuant to CCP § 473(b). The former president of the dissolved corporation mistakenly thought there could be no liability because the corporation was dissolved and had no assets. A reasonable and honest mistake of the law is grounds for granting relief under CCP § 473(b). (Security Truck Line v. City of Monterey (1953) 117 Cal.App.2d 441, 445-46.) Defendant's motion to set aside default is timely. Plaintiff will not be prejudiced by setting aside the default. Defendant has filed a proposed answer, no depositions have been taken yet, and defendant is not seeking to postpone the trial date which is currently set in July 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KENNETH J. RADER JR VS. SUN LIFE ASSURANCE COMPANY OF CANADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Sun Life'S Deposition Subpoenas Seeking The Production Of Private Credit Card Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 4 - PLAINTIFF KENNETH RADER's Motion To Quash Sun Life'S Deposition Subpoenas Seeking The Production Of Private Credit Card Records. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. In this litigation Plaintiff alleges that he became disabled from his occupation as a Code Enforcement Officer for the City of Brentwood on or about July 9, 2010. Sun Life contends that Plaintiff's disability was not medically documented until September of 2011, after Plaintiff's employment with the City of Brentwood, and his Sun Life coverage, had ended. Plaintiff's day-to-day activities and functionality both before and after his employment ended are of key relevance to the issue of when and to what extend Plaintiff became disabled. Plaintiff's credit card records from January 1, 2008 to present provide directly-relevant evidence of those activities that may not be available from other sources. This order is without prejudice to Plaintiff's right to designate any such records for protection under the protective order entered in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JI YANG et al VS. HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED, DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents From Deft And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 5 - PLAINTIFF JI YANG, ALELI SAN JUAN, IRMANETTE DE ROSAS, MILENN MARIANO and MARLYN DATAR's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents From Deft And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The documents requested by Plaintiffs concerning the treatment of Resident X and Resident X's medical condition are directly relevant to Plaintiff's claims. Defendant's alleged reasons for terminating Plaintiffs center on the assertion that Plaintiffs' alleged misconduct resulted in adverse health effects on Resident X, and Plaintiffs assert that Defendant's reasons are a pretext for alleged discrimination against Plaintiffs. The privacy interests of Resident X can be protected by designation of the produced documents as protected under the protective order in place in this litigation and by redaction of Resident X's identity in the documents produced. Within 30 days Defendant shall produce documents responsive to Request for Production Nos. 15, 21, 23, 26 (Defendant may comply by producing summary lists of employees of Defendant who treated or provided care for the resident who was the subject of Plaintiffs' terminations shall suffice); 31, 34, 37, 43, 47, 49, 50, 56, 57 (Defendant may comply by producing summary documents listing publicly-available information about the lawsuits or complaints, and copies of any pleadings in the possession of Defendant or its attorneys), 58, 61, 68, and 69. Compelling production of documents in response to Request for Production no. 60 would compel Defendant's attorneys to reveal their mental processes and work product. Therefore, Plaintiff's motion is DENIED with respect to Request No. 60. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. JUANA M CALERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Hearing required. No opposition was filed. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant owes $2,559.81 in damages. In the Answer to the Complaint, filed September 17, 2013, Defendant has checked the box that "Defendant admits that all of the statement of the complaint or cross-complaint are correct." Plaintiff states in his moving papers that Defendant has no valid affirmative defense. (Motion at 3.) However, Defendant has not attached a copy of the pleading at issue (that is, the Answer) with its moving papers, and the copy of the Answer scanned on the Court's Register of Actions may be incomplete; it shows "page 1 of 2," but not any second page of the Answer. Thus, the Court cannot determine whether any affirmative defense was pled. The moving party must bring a file stamped copy of the Answer to the hearing by 9:15 a.m. on the morning of the hearing, or, if possible, email it to the court as soon as possible this afternoon at contestdept302tr@sftc.org. See Local Rule 2.6(C). =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH K ROBINS VS. DIGNITY HEALTH FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH FOUNDATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Granted, with leave to amend from notice of entry of order. No opposition was filed. The demurrer to the entire Complaint is sustained for uncertainty (CCP 430.10(f)) and failure to state facts that constitute a cause of action (CCP 430.10(e)). Plaintiff is given 15 days leave to amend to state a cause of action against defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. TAMRA WIENKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 26, 2013, Line 22, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC.'s Application For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied without prejudice. The moving party has failed to attach a proof of service in accordance with Cal. Rule of Court 3.1300(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
